sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The EventTemplate class is a Cython-implemented class within the AnalysisG framework, serving as a foundational template for event-based data structures. It provides a high-performance interface to an underlying C++ event object, enabling efficient data manipulation and access within the Python environment. This class is designed to be inherited by more specific event implementations, providing common functionalities for event indexing, weighting, and tree/branch management.

sect1.title: The EventTemplate Class
sect1.para: The EventTemplate is a cdef class, indicating it's a Cython extension type, allowing direct interaction with C++ objects and types for performance optimization. It encapsulates a pointer to a C++ event_template object, managing its lifecycle and exposing its properties and methods to Python.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): The Cython "constructor" equivalent. This method is called when the object is allocated. If the instance is not EventTemplate itself (i.e., it's a subclass), it returns immediately. Otherwise, it allocates a new C++ event_template object and assigns its pointer to self.ptr.
__init__(self): The Python initializer. This method is intentionally left empty, as initialization logic is handled by __cinit__ and the underlying C++ object.

__dealloc__(self): The Cython "destructor" equivalent. This method is called when the object is deallocated. If the instance is not EventTemplate itself, it returns. Otherwise, it deallocates the C++ event_template object pointed to by self.ptr, preventing memory leaks.
__name__(self): Returns the name of the underlying C++ event object as a Python string. It uses the env utility function to convert a C++ string to a Python string.
__hash__(self): Computes a hash value for the event. It converts the first 8 characters of the C++ object's hash string to an integer.

__eq__(self, other): Compares two EventTemplate objects for equality. It first checks if other is an instance or subclass of EventTemplate using is_self. If so, it compares the underlying C++ event_template objects.
is_self(self, inpt) -> bool: A utility method to check if the given input inpt is an instance of EventTemplate or a subclass thereof.

sect2.title: Properties
sect2.para: The following properties provide controlled access to the underlying C++ event_template's data members.

listitem.para:
@property index -> int: Retrieves the integer index of the event.
@index.setter index(self, val: Union[str, double]): Sets the index of the event. It attempts to set the C++ index directly. If a TypeError occurs (e.g., val is a string), it assumes val represents a leaf name and stores it in self.ptr.leaves.

@property weight -> double: Retrieves the double-precision floating-point weight of the event.
@weight.setter weight(self, val: Union[str, double]): Sets the weight of the event. Similar to index.setter, it handles TypeError by storing val in self.ptr.leaves.

@property Tree -> str: Retrieves the name of the primary ROOT tree associated with the event.
@Tree.setter Tree(self, str val): Sets the name of the primary ROOT tree. It uses the enc utility function to convert the Python string val to a C++ string.

@property Trees -> list: Retrieves a list of all ROOT tree names associated with the event.
@Trees.setter Trees(self, val: Union[str, list]): Sets the list of ROOT tree names. It can accept a single string or a list of strings. If a list, it iterates and calls the setter for each element.

@property Branches -> list: Retrieves a list of all ROOT branch names associated with the event.
@Branches.setter Branches(self, val: Union[str, list]): Sets the list of ROOT branch names. Similar to Trees.setter, it handles single strings or lists of strings.
@property Name: Retrieves the name of the underlying C++ event object. This appears to be a duplicate of __name__ but exposed as a property.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
The EventTemplate class heavily relies on Cython's ability to interact with C++.
cdef class EventTemplate: Declares EventTemplate as a Cython extension type.
cdef event_template *ptr: A C++ pointer to an event_template object, managed by the Cython class.
from libcpp cimport int, vector: Imports C++ primitive types and containers.
from AnalysisG.core.event_template cimport *: Imports C++ declarations from the corresponding .pxd file.
from AnalysisG.core.tools cimport *: Imports C++ declarations from the tools.pxd file, likely containing env and enc utility functions for string conversion.
env(self.ptr.name) and enc(val): These functions (presumably from AnalysisG.core.tools) handle the conversion between C++ std::string and Python str.
